# gcore_cloud_inference_deployment (Resource)

Inference deployments run containerized ML models with configurable scaling, health probes, and GPU flavors.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "gcore_cloud_inference_deployment" "example_cloud_inference_deployment" {
  project_id = 1
  containers = [{
    region_id = 1
    scale = {
      max = 3
      min = 1
      cooldown_period = 60
      polling_interval = 30
      triggers = {
        cpu = {
          threshold = 80
        }
        gpu_memory = {
          threshold = 80
        }
        gpu_utilization = {
          threshold = 80
        }
        http = {
          rate = 1
          window = 60
        }
        memory = {
          threshold = 70
        }
        sqs = {
          activation_queue_length = 1
          aws_region = "us-east-1"
          queue_length = 10
          queue_url = "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/MyQueue"
          secret_name = "x"
          aws_endpoint = "aws_endpoint"
          scale_on_delayed = true
          scale_on_flight = true
        }
      }
    }
  }]
  flavor_name = "inference-16vcpu-232gib-1xh100-80gb"
  image = "nginx:latest"
  listening_port = 80
  name = "my-instance"
  api_keys = ["key1", "key2"]
  auth_enabled = false
  command = ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
  credentials_name = "dockerhub"
  description = "My first instance"
  envs = {
    DEBUG_MODE = "False"
    KEY = "12345"
  }
  ingress_opts = {
    disable_response_buffering = true
  }
  logging = {
    destination_region_id = 1
    enabled = true
    retention_policy = {
      period = 42
    }
    topic_name = "my-log-name"
  }
  probes = {
    liveness_probe = {
      enabled = true
      probe = {
        exec = {
          command = ["ls", "-l"]
        }
        failure_threshold = 3
        http_get = {
          port = 80
          headers = {
            Authorization = "Bearer token 123"
          }
          host = "127.0.0.1"
          path = "/healthz"
          schema = "HTTP"
        }
        initial_delay_seconds = 0
        period_seconds = 5
        success_threshold = 1
        tcp_socket = {
          port = 80
        }
        timeout_seconds = 1
      }
    }
    readiness_probe = {
      enabled = true
      probe = {
        exec = {
          command = ["ls", "-l"]
        }
        failure_threshold = 3
        http_get = {
          port = 80
          headers = {
            Authorization = "Bearer token 123"
          }
          host = "127.0.0.1"
          path = "/healthz"
          schema = "HTTP"
        }
        initial_delay_seconds = 0
        period_seconds = 5
        success_threshold = 1
        tcp_socket = {
          port = 80
        }
        timeout_seconds = 1
      }
    }
    startup_probe = {
      enabled = true
      probe = {
        exec = {
          command = ["ls", "-l"]
        }
        failure_threshold = 3
        http_get = {
          port = 80
          headers = {
            Authorization = "Bearer token 123"
          }
          host = "127.0.0.1"
          path = "/healthz"
          schema = "HTTP"
        }
        initial_delay_seconds = 0
        period_seconds = 5
        success_threshold = 1
        tcp_socket = {
          port = 80
        }
        timeout_seconds = 1
      }
    }
  }
  timeout = 120
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `containers` (Attributes List) List of containers for the inference instance. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers))
- `flavor_name` (String) Flavor name for the inference instance.
- `image` (String) Docker image for the inference instance. This field should contain the image name and tag in the format 'name:tag', e.g., 'nginx:latest'. It defaults to Docker Hub as the image registry, but any accessible Docker image URL can be specified.
- `listening_port` (Number) Listening port for the inference instance.
- `name` (String) Inference instance name.

### Optional

- `api_keys` (List of String) List of API keys for the inference instance. Multiple keys can be attached to one deployment.If `auth_enabled` and `api_keys` are both specified, a ValidationError will be raised.
- `auth_enabled` (Boolean, Deprecated) Set to `true` to enable API key authentication for the inference instance. `"Authorization": "Bearer *****"` or `"X-Api-Key": "*****"` header is required for the requests to the instance if enabled. This field is deprecated and will be removed in the future. Use `api_keys` field instead.If `auth_enabled` and `api_keys` are both specified, a ValidationError will be raised.
- `command` (List of String) Command to be executed when running a container from an image.
- `credentials_name` (String) Registry credentials name
- `description` (String) Inference instance description.
- `envs` (Map of String) Environment variables for the inference instance.
- `ingress_opts` (Attributes) Ingress options for the inference instance (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ingress_opts))
- `logging` (Attributes) Logging configuration for the inference instance (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--logging))
- `probes` (Attributes) Probes configured for all containers of the inference instance. If probes are not provided, and the `image_name` is from a the Model Catalog registry, the default probes will be used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes))
- `project_id` (Number) Project ID
- `timeout` (Number) Specifies the duration in seconds without any requests after which the containers will be downscaled to their minimum scale value as defined by `scale.min`. If set, this helps in optimizing resource usage by reducing the number of container instances during periods of inactivity. The default value when the parameter is not set is 120.

### Read-Only

- `address` (String) Address of the inference instance
- `created_at` (String) Inference instance creation date in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) Inference instance name.
- `object_references` (Attributes List) Indicates to which parent object this inference belongs to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--object_references))
- `status` (String) Inference instance status.

Value can be one of the following:
- `DEPLOYING` - The instance is being deployed. Containers are not yet created.
- `PARTIALLYDEPLOYED` - All containers have been created, but some may not be ready yet. Instances stuck in this state typically indicate either image being pulled, or a failure of some kind. In the latter case, the `error_message` field of the respective container object in the `containers` collection explains the failure reason.
- `ACTIVE` - The instance is running and ready to accept requests.
- `DISABLED` - The instance is disabled and not accepting any requests.
- `PENDING` - The instance is running but scaled to zero. It will be automatically scaled up when a request is made.
- `DELETING` - The instance is being deleted.
Available values: "ACTIVE", "DELETING", "DEPLOYING", "DISABLED", "PARTIALLYDEPLOYED", "PENDING".
- `tasks` (List of String) List of task IDs representing asynchronous operations. Use these IDs to monitor operation progress:
- `GET /v1/tasks/{task_id}` - Check individual task status and details
Poll task status until completion (`FINISHED`/`ERROR`) before proceeding with dependent operations.

<a id="nestedatt--containers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers`

Required:

- `region_id` (Number) Region id for the container
- `scale` (Attributes) Scale for the container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale))

<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale`

Required:

- `max` (Number) Maximum scale for the container
- `min` (Number) Minimum scale for the container

Optional:

- `cooldown_period` (Number) Cooldown period between scaling actions in seconds
- `polling_interval` (Number) Polling interval for scaling triggers in seconds
- `triggers` (Attributes) Triggers for scaling actions (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers))

<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers`

Optional:

- `cpu` (Attributes) CPU trigger configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--cpu))
- `gpu_memory` (Attributes) GPU memory trigger configuration. Calculated by `DCGM_FI_DEV_MEM_COPY_UTIL` metric (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--gpu_memory))
- `gpu_utilization` (Attributes) GPU utilization trigger configuration. Calculated by `DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL` metric (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--gpu_utilization))
- `http` (Attributes) HTTP trigger configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--http))
- `memory` (Attributes) Memory trigger configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--memory))
- `sqs` (Attributes) SQS trigger configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--sqs))

<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--cpu"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `threshold` (Number) Threshold value for the trigger in percentage


<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--gpu_memory"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `threshold` (Number) Threshold value for the trigger in percentage


<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--gpu_utilization"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `threshold` (Number) Threshold value for the trigger in percentage


<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--http"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `rate` (Number) Request count per 'window' seconds for the http trigger
- `window` (Number) Time window for rate calculation in seconds


<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--memory"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `threshold` (Number) Threshold value for the trigger in percentage


<a id="nestedatt--containers--scale--triggers--sqs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `containers.scale.triggers.sqs`

Required:

- `activation_queue_length` (Number) Number of messages for activation
- `aws_region` (String) AWS region
- `queue_length` (Number) Number of messages for one replica
- `queue_url` (String) SQS queue URL
- `secret_name` (String) Auth secret name

Optional:

- `aws_endpoint` (String) Custom AWS endpoint
- `scale_on_delayed` (Boolean) Scale on delayed messages
- `scale_on_flight` (Boolean) Scale on in-flight messages





<a id="nestedatt--ingress_opts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ingress_opts`

Optional:

- `disable_response_buffering` (Boolean) Disable response buffering if true. A client usually has a much slower connection and can not consume the response data as fast as it is produced by an upstream application. Ingress tries to buffer the whole response in order to release the upstream application as soon as possible.By default, the response buffering is enabled.


<a id="nestedatt--logging"></a>
### Nested Schema for `logging`

Optional:

- `destination_region_id` (Number) ID of the region in which the logs will be stored
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable or disable log streaming
- `retention_policy` (Attributes) Logs retention policy (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--logging--retention_policy))
- `topic_name` (String) The topic name to stream logs to

<a id="nestedatt--logging--retention_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `logging.retention_policy`

Required:

- `period` (Number) Duration of days for which logs must be kept.



<a id="nestedatt--probes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes`

Optional:

- `liveness_probe` (Attributes) Liveness probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe))
- `readiness_probe` (Attributes) Readiness probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--readiness_probe))
- `startup_probe` (Attributes) Startup probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--startup_probe))

<a id="nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes.liveness_probe`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the probe is enabled or not.

Optional:

- `probe` (Attributes) Probe configuration (exec, `http_get` or `tcp_socket`) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe))

<a id="nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes.liveness_probe.probe`

Optional:

- `exec` (Attributes) Exec probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--exec))
- `failure_threshold` (Number) The number of consecutive probe failures that mark the container as unhealthy.
- `http_get` (Attributes) HTTP GET probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--http_get))
- `initial_delay_seconds` (Number) The initial delay before starting the first probe.
- `period_seconds` (Number) How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
- `success_threshold` (Number) The number of consecutive successful probes that mark the container as healthy.
- `tcp_socket` (Attributes) TCP socket probe configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--tcp_socket))
- `timeout_seconds` (Number) The timeout for each probe.

<a id="nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--exec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes.liveness_probe.probe.timeout_seconds`

Required:

- `command` (List of String) Command to be executed inside the running container.


<a id="nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--http_get"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes.liveness_probe.probe.timeout_seconds`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number the probe should connect to.

Optional:

- `headers` (Map of String) HTTP headers to be sent with the request.
- `host` (String) Host name to send HTTP request to.
- `path` (String) The endpoint to send the HTTP request to.
- `schema` (String) Schema to use for the HTTP request.


<a id="nestedatt--probes--liveness_probe--probe--tcp_socket"></a>
### Nested Schema for `probes.liveness_probe.probe.timeout_seconds`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number to check if it's open.

<a id="nestedatt--object_references"></a>
### Nested Schema for `object_references`

Read-Only:

- `kind` (String) Kind of the inference object to be referenced
Available values: "AppDeployment".
- `name` (String) Name of the inference object to be referenced

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
$ terraform import gcore_cloud_inference_deployment.example '<project_id>/<deployment_name>'
```
